policy_refs: [BNK-CC-RULE-001, BNK-CC-RULE-002, BNK-CC-RULE-003, BNK-CC-RULE-004, BNK-CC-RULE-007, BNK-CC-RULE-010, BNK-SEC-RULE-001, BNK-SEC-RULE-003, BNK-REG-RULE-012, BNK-FRAUD-RULE-007, BNK-CC-RULE-020, BNK-CC-RULE-021, BNK-CC-RULE-022, BNK-CC-RULE-023]
expected_outcome: Business customer wants to wire $5,000 to a domestic supplier. Sarah completes Enhanced Verification (5-of-5 identifiers + callback to phone-on-file) per BNK-CC-RULE-007, runs OFAC on the beneficiary per BNK-REG-RULE-012, asks elder-exploitation probing questions per BNK-FRAUD-RULE-007 (precautionary), processes the wire. PASS — exemplary high-risk-transaction handling. Customer mildly impatient with the verification depth but ultimately appreciates it.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Thank you for calling Meridian Trust Bank. My name is Sarah, how may I help you today?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Please note this call is recorded for quality assurance and regulatory compliance purposes.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Sarah, this is Walt Henderson. I need to send a wire to a supplier today — five thousand dollars, domestic.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Hello, Mr. Henderson. I can absolutely help with that today.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: I understand you are calling regarding an outbound wire transfer. I can certainly help you with that. Wires are an elevated-risk transaction, so I'll need to take you through our Enhanced Verification process — that's a few more identifiers than a routine call, and a callback to the phone on file. Are you on the phone number we have on file with you?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: I am, yes. Same number you have.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Wonderful. Let's start with the standard identifiers. Could you give me your account number?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Four-four-seven-one, eight-eight-two-six, zero-nine.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Thank you. Your date of birth?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: July sixth, nineteen-seventy-one.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: And the last four of your Social?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: One-zero-seven-two.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Full legal name on the account?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Walter Edward Henderson.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: That matches. You're at four of five for standard verification.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: What's the fifth?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Your security challenge phrase — the answer you set when you opened the account.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Cedar Lake. The town we used to summer in.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: That's correct. Now, for the Enhanced Verification piece specific to the wire — I'm going to hang up and call you back on the phone number on file, which is ending in two-eight-zero-zero. When I do, please answer and confirm a one-time code I'm about to read to you now. The code is seven-four-nine-three. Just hold it for me.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Okay, seven-four-nine-three. Is the callback really necessary for five grand?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Honestly, yes — this is exactly the kind of small-but-not-trivial wire scammers use against business customers, and the callback is the one step that defeats most of those attempts. I know it's an extra minute. I'd rather use that minute and have you safe.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Fair enough. Go ahead.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Hanging up briefly. Talk to you in fifteen seconds.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Hello. Walt Henderson again.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Hi, Mr. Henderson — Sarah at Meridian. Could you read me back the one-time code I gave you?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Seven-four-nine-three.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Perfect. Enhanced Verification complete.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Good.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Now, for the wire details. I'll capture the beneficiary account number through secure keypad entry rather than asking you to read it aloud. Could you tell me the beneficiary's full name first?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Halverson Industrial Supply Incorporated.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Beneficiary bank?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: First Continental in Cleveland Ohio.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Thank you. Before I run the screening — a couple of routine questions that we ask on every wire. Has anyone outside your business asked you to send this wire, or is anyone listening in or directing you on this call right now?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: No, nobody. Halverson is a vendor I've worked with for fourteen years. This is an invoice payment.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Wonderful. I asked because we're required to ask — it catches the rare case where someone is being coerced. Running the beneficiary against the OFAC sanctions list now.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Sure.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: I'm still here with you, Mr. Henderson — just running the screen. Should be about ten seconds.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: OFAC screen complete, no match. Beneficiary clears.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: For the disclosures — the outbound domestic wire fee is twenty-five dollars, debited from the funding account. The wire is irrevocable once sent. Settlement is typically same-day if sent before five PM Eastern; we are well within that window. You will receive a confirmation number once the wire transmits.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Understood. Twenty-five dollar fee, irrevocable, same-day. Please proceed.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Funding from your business checking ending zero-nine, five thousand dollars principal plus twenty-five dollar fee, beneficiary Halverson Industrial Supply at First Continental. Confirming you authorize?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Confirmed.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Submitting the wire now.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Wire is accepted. Confirmation number is W-E-I dash three-three-eight-two-four-one-zero. You'll see the debit on your account immediately and the funds should reach Halverson by end of business today.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: To summarize: I completed Enhanced Verification including the callback, ran OFAC on the beneficiary, processed the five-thousand-dollar wire to Halverson Industrial Supply at First Continental, twenty-five-dollar fee debited, confirmation number provided. Same-day settlement expected. Does that all sound correct?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Is there anything else I can help you with today, Mr. Henderson?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: No, that covers it. And I'll say — I gave you a hard time about the callback, but I appreciate that you do it. Better safe than sorry.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Thank you for saying that — it makes a real difference. Your case reference number is M-T-B dash two-two-six-six-zero-five-three. You'll receive a brief survey after we hang up. Thank you for banking with Meridian, Mr. Henderson.
